dossier.overview:
Pokémon Resort's overworld follower is a single companion Pokémon that walks behind the player on the 3D test map (RESORT → 3D TEST). When the player stands still, nature-driven idle behaviours kick in: weighted random actions such as circling, poking, sleeping, or exploring within a radius of the player.

Config lives under config/gameplay/followers/. Runtime code is in src/gameplay/world3d/followers/ — planner separate from executor, same pattern planned for future map-spawned Pokémon.

sections.title: Follower guide
sections.summary: How summoning works, how idle behaviours are chosen, and every behaviour id available today.

blocks.body:
The follower prototype is wired through Overworld3DTestScreen: it loads session.json (which species, nature, optional forced behaviour), summon.json (ball throw + follow step timing), and idle_behaviours.json (nature weights and motion tuning).

Lifecycle states: Hidden → BallRelease → EntryFlash → Active. While Active and the player is moving, the follower pathfinds to the tile the player last stood on. When the player is idle for startAfterIdleSeconds (default 5 s), the NatureIdlePlanner picks a behaviour from weighted tables and FollowerController executes a queue of small action primitives.

Player movement cancels idle immediately and runs cancel_return — the follower pathfinds back to an adjacent tile near the player (or soft-snaps after a delay). Enable enable_active_idle_behavior_debug in config/app.json to show the active behaviour label on screen (AIB: …).
Captured from RESORT → 3D TEST (pokemon-resort/screenshots/overworld3d_test.webp). Follower active on the test owmap with debug idle label when enabled.
blocks.caption: Overworld 3D test — follower on map

blocks.title: Idle behaviour pipeline
blocks.caption: Planning vs execution
blocks.source:
flowchart LR
    CFG[idle_behaviours.json] --> PLAN[NatureIdlePlanner]
    SESS[session.json nature] --> PLAN
    PLAN -->|IdlePlan actions| EXEC[FollowerController]
    EXEC -->|MovePath Face Wait| WORLD[Grid pathfinding]
    EXEC -->|Poke Jump Sleep| ANIM[Sprite + dust VFX]

blocks.caption: Session config — species, nature, optional forced behaviour for testing

blocks.body:
Summon timing (summon.json):

• followStepDurationMs — grid step duration while catching up (default 250 ms)
• ballAnimation — duration, full frame strip, optional fall from fallHeightWorld
• entryAnimation — scale-in flash when the Pokémon appears

Normal follow: each time the player enters a new tile, the follower builds a path to the player's previous tile using buildFollowerPath. Pathfinding respects collision, height deltas (±1), ramp special tiles, NPC occupancy, and treats the player's current tile as occupied unless it is the goal.

Charbin resolution: resolveFollowerPokemonCharbinPath and resolveFollowerPokeballCharbinPath map species and ball ids to assets/characters/pokemon/*.charbin packages.

How a behaviour is chosen:

1. Read nature from session (e.g. Jolly) → map to a weight group (assertive, steady, curious, composed, energetic, neutral).
2. Start with groupWeights[group] for each behaviour id (0–10 after clamp).
3. Add natureModifiers[nature] deltas (can be negative).
4. Drop behaviours with weight ≤ 0. Sleep is skipped unless sleep is available (not yet enabled in production planner calls).
5. Quirky nature: 20% chance to flatten all weights to 1 for a random pick.
6. Weighted random select → NatureIdlePlanner builds an IdlePlan (10–20 s target duration by default).
7. On natural end, optionally path back to idle origin tile at returnToOriginSpeedMultiplier (1.35×).

If planning fails (blocked tiles), fallbackOrder runs: watch_player → face_away → random_walk → do_nothing.

forcedBehavior in session.json overrides weights so only that id can win (used for QA and captures).

blocks.html:
Config idWhat the follower doesAction primitives
random_walkPicks random walkable tiles within maxPlayerRadius of the player; pauses between legs.MovePath, Wait
random_exploreLike random walk but turns to random facings and shorter glances between steps.MovePath, Face, Wait
watch_playerStays in place; alternates facing the player and glancing to random directions.Face, Wait
approach_player_sidePathfinds to an adjacent tile beside the player, then faces the player with long pauses.MovePath, Face, Wait
circle_playerWalks the ring of tiles around the player (cardinal neighbours).MovePath
dance_circleOrbits like circle_player; 25% chance each step to do a full spin sequence.MovePath, Face, Wait
spin_orbitFaster orbit (1.15× speed) with rapid random facing changes.MovePath, Face
poke_playerApproaches adjacent tile, then repeated forward poke nudges toward the player (1–3 per burst).MovePath, Face, Poke
poke_in_placePoke bursts without moving; faces player between bursts.Face, Poke, Wait
sleepPlays sleep animation for at least 3 s (requires sleep_available — currently false in live planner).Sleep
face_awayTurns back to the player and holds for the behaviour duration.Face, Wait
drift_awayWalks to a tile 2+ steps from player (preferring farther), then faces away with pauses.MovePath, Face, Wait
guard_postLike drift_away but picks closer tiles (min radius 1); holds facing away — sentinel posture.MovePath, Face, Wait
inspect_poiRandom walk within radius with explore-style turning — stands in for POI inspection until map POIs exist.MovePath, Face, Wait
jump_fidgetRepeated in-place jumps (1–4 per burst) with landing dust VFX when enabled.Jump, Wait
hop_wanderShort hops between nearby tiles (hop movement flag on path steps).MovePath (hop), Wait
shy_hideSteps to player's side/back tile if valid, then alternates facing toward and away from player.MovePath, Face, Wait
follow_npcRandom walk within radius (same pathing as random_walk today; NPC targeting reserved).MovePath, Wait
do_nothingFallback only — no actions.—

Weight groupNaturesTypical lean
assertiveLonely, Brave, Adamant, Naughtydrift_away, guard_post, poke
steadyBold, Relaxed, Impish, Laxsleep, guard_post, calm pacing
curiousModest, Mild, Quiet, Rashinspect_poi, random_explore
composedCalm, Gentle, Sassy, Carefulface_away, guard_post, inspect_poi
energeticTimid, Hasty, Jolly, Naivecircle, dance, jump, hop — sleep weight 0
neutralHardy, Docile, Serious, Bashful, Quirkybalanced; Quirky gets random roll

blocks.body: Per-behaviour GIFs (idle-watch-player, idle-circle-player, etc.) are still pending. Set forcedBehavior in session.json, stand still 5+ s, and record each behaviour separately when capturing.

Global idle tuning in idle_behaviours.json → natureIdle:

• startAfterIdleSeconds — delay before first behaviour (5.0)
• behaviorDurationSeconds — min/max plan length (10–20 s)
• cooldownBetweenBehaviorsSeconds — gap before next pick (2.5–4 s)
• maxPlayerRadius — tile Manhattan radius for random targets (5)
• movementSpeedMultiplier / returnToOriginSpeedMultiplier / cancelReturnSpeedMultiplier
• restoreOriginalPositionOnNaturalEnd / restoreOriginalDirectionOnNaturalEnd
• jump — heightPixels (16), durationSeconds (0.35)
• poke — distanceTiles, forwardSeconds, returnSeconds
• landingDust — dust.png sprite sheet on jump landings
• behaviorWeightClamp — weights clamped to 0–10
• quirkyRandomBehaviorChance — 0.2

blocks.caption: Core idle timing and motion multipliers

blocks.html:
FilePurpose
config/gameplay/followers/session.jsonEnabled flag, species, ball, nature, forcedBehavior
config/gameplay/followers/summon.jsonBall throw, entry flash, follow step ms
config/gameplay/followers/idle_behaviours.jsonWeights, nature modifiers, jump/poke/dust
config/app.jsonenable_active_idle_behavior_debug overlay

sections.title: Code map
sections.summary: Modules to read when extending follower or idle behaviour.

blocks.html:
ModuleRole
NatureIdleConfig.cppLoads idle_behaviours.json
NatureIdlePlanner.cppWeight selection + IdlePlan action queues
FollowerController.cppSummon states, follow, idle execution, cancel return
FollowerIdleExitTarget.cppPick adjacent tile when returning after idle
FollowerConfig.cppLoads session.json + summon.json; charbin paths
Overworld3DTestScreen.cppScreen adapter — wires player idle/activity into follower

blocks.caption: Nature → weight group mapping

blocks.body:
Action primitive types (IdleActionType): MovePath, Wait, Face, Poke, Jump, Sleep. The planner never touches SDL — it only emits plans. FollowerController translates grid paths to world steps, drives the charbin animator, and spawns landing dust via LandingDustSystem on jump landings.

This follower is explicitly a single-actor, player-relative prototype. Future overworld Pokémon (multi-actor, spawn points, smart objects) should reuse the planner/executor split but not grow FollowerController into general AI — see docs/gameplay/overworld_pokemon_architecture_handoff.md in the game repo.

Recording tip: set forcedBehavior in session.json, launch RESORT → 3D TEST, stand still 5+ s. Source screenshots live in pokemon-resort/screenshots/ and are copied to public/media/docs/pokemon-resort/ for the site.

In-repo (pokemon-resort/):

• docs/gameplay/overworld_pokemon_architecture_handoff.md — boundaries for future map-spawned Pokémon
• docs/gameplay/owmap_format.md — overworld map binary loaded by the test screen

Resort Docs:

• owmap-format — map editor write/read path
• charbin-schema — follower sprite packages (.charbin)
• island-dreamforge — terrain authoring upstream of maps
